name: Sancocho
recipeInstructions:
There are many versions of this stew-soup in Central and South America. Scented with cumin, it makes a hearty supper on a chilly winter night.
1. In a medium nonstick saucepan, heat the oil over medium-high heat.
2. Add chicken; cook, stirring frequently, until lightly browned on all sides.
3. Remove chicken and set aside.
4. Add onion; cook until golden.
5. Add celery, bell pepper, jalapeno, and cumin; cook, stirring, for 3 minutes.
6. Add broth, tomatoes, pumpkin, and sweet potato; bring to a boil.
7. Reduce heat, cover, and simmer until the sweet potato is tender.
8. Add reserved chicken and corn; simmer for an additional 10 minutes.
9. Sprinkle with cilantro and serve with rice.
Each serving provides:
1/4 Fat, 2 Vitamins, 1 Protein, 1-1/2 Carbs, 15 Calories
Per serving:
305 calories, 16 grams protein, 50 grams carbohydrates
4 grams fat: 1 gram polyunsaturated, 1 gram monounsaturated, 1 gram saturated
102 milligrams sodium, 30 milligrams cholesterol
Note: For best flavor, use small sugar pumpkins (about 2-3 pounds). Cut in half, remove seeds and strings with a large spoon, peel and dice. Or you can bake the pumpkin whole in a roasting pan at 375°F for about 1 hour, or until it can be easily pierced with a knife. Allow to cool; remove seeds and strings, and scoop out flesh with a spoon. You may use prebaked or canned pumpkin in this recipe too.
Source: Wonderful World of Walnuts & Rice (Weight Watchers Magazine in association with The Rice Council and The Walnut Marketing Board)
